title: alcea aucheri
intro: Alcea aucheri, also known as the Syrian hollyhock or Persian hollyhock, is a plant species in the mallow family (Malvaceae) that is native to the Middle East and Central Asia. The medicinal properties of Alcea aucheri include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to its alkaloids and flavonoids. The health benefits of consuming Alcea aucheri include reducing fever, soothing coughs, and alleviating digestive issues, as well as its potential to act as an immune system booster. Alcea aucheri is traditionally prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and its roots can be dried and used in powdered form for medicinal purposes.
